description: Escalating tensions between the U.S. and Iran in the Gulf region have resulted in a drop in both the S&P 500 and Nasdaq, fueled by a spike in oil prices. Iranian closure of the Strait of Hormuz heightened fears, affecting chip stocks and raising concerns over potential inflation risks.
content:

title: Retail Inflation up to 4.4% on costlier food, transport
description: India's retail inflation reached 4.4% in June, surpassing the central bank's target. Higher food and transport costs drove this increase, marking the highest print under the new CPI series. Food inflation accelerated to 5.3%, crossing the five percent mark for the first time. Core inflation moderated to 3.9%, partly due to falling gold and silver prices. Economists anticipate the Reserve Bank of India will maintain its current policy rate.

Additionally, India's merchandise trade deficit saw a notable increase in June, raising concerns about persistent inflation that might lead to interest rate hikes.

title: India's June retail inflation quickens to 4.38%, crosses RBI's 4% target after 17 months
description: India's retail inflation reached 4.38 percent in June, exceeding the central bank's target. Elevated food and fuel prices contributed to this significant acceleration in price pressures. The Reserve Bank of India maintained its benchmark repo rate at 5.25 percent during its review. Policymakers are balancing inflation risks with the need to support economic growth. Concerns remain about global energy prices and an erratic monsoon season.

title: FM Sitharaman to Meet PSB Chiefs to Review Foreign Currency Deposit Drive
description: Finance Minister Nirmala Sitharaman will meet the heads of public sector banks on July 13 to review the progress of the foreign currency deposit mobilisation campaign aimed at attracting overseas funds. The review comes after the Reserve Bank of India relaxed interest rate norms for FCNR(B) deposits and introduced concessional forex swap facilities to encourage banks and public sector enterprises to raise foreign currency resources. The meeting is also expected to focus on external commercial borrowings and credit growth, with the government likely to encourage banks to increase lending to productive sectors of the economy.
